programme.title: Bill Bailey: Cosmic Jam
programme.desc: Bill demonstrates the influence of Cockney music on the classical greats and warns of a world ruled by insects, in his debut live stand-up show filmed at the Bloomsbury Theatre in 1996.

programme.title: Bargain Holiday Secrets
programme.desc: Sun, sea, sand and saving money? Yes please! Jasmine reveals the industry tricks to an indulgent beach break for less, from the inside scoop on new resorts to cutting the cost of package trips.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Double trouble George and Daisy cant bear to live apart, but does anyone have a garden big enough for both of them? And will Bailey the Brave live up to his name at last, and give would-be owner Ruth the chance to show him the world?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit this week is to Knockaloe Beg farm on the Isle of Man, where owners Fiona and John Anderson hope their guests will embrace their green ethos. Blackpool BandBers Robbie and John Welling are surprised that it is so clean for a farm.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to The Arches on the Isle of Man where ex-demolition man Phil Routledge is proud of his super-modern BandB, which was voted the best in the Isle of Man. On arrival, his guests are blown away by the views.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to the Trafford in Blackpool owned by couple Robbie and John who are proud of their traditional bucket and spade BandB. On arrival the guests are pleasantly surprised, even if previous host Phil is hell-bent on finding some dust.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The last visit of the week is to the grand Sheepy Lodge in Warwickshire where empty-nester Julie loves to fill her manor house with people - as long as they dont order poached eggs.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the last day of the competition and the rival BandB owners meet to settle some scores and discover what theyve been paid. Kicking things off is Knockaloe Beg farm on the Isle of Man, and hosts Fiona and John Anderson tackle criticisms.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at the Three Horseshoes in Norfolk, where married couple Fiona and Michael hope to gallop to victory with the homely vibes at their traditional country pub. But someones shower leaves them feeling dour.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Carl and Liz host the weeks second visit at The Red House in Cambridgeshire. Their coaching inn with converted stable bedrooms leaves the BandBers with some positive first impressions - until one pair of guests give their drawers a good dusting down.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks third visit is to Barbara and Elizabeths Holly Lodge Boutique BandB in Norfolk. Some doubts about grout get things off to a bad start, but the hosts dance into the good books with an afternoon soundtracked by an old-school Wurlitzer organ.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The weeks final visit is to Helen and Carls self-catering glampsite, Peacock Lake, in Nottinghamshire. The hosts feedback has ruffled plenty of feathers throughout the week, so their guests expectations are sky-high.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Cleanliness has been the buzz word this week, with pretty much all of the hosts unhappy about their less-than-sparkling scores. Have some of them been playing dirty?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features an Austrian moped.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. Simon and Max visit a house stuck in the 1950s.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Leah Charles-King helps Sarah and Andy find the perfect place for their whole family, including their beloved dog, Ruby, on Spains southern Costa Blanca, for their budget of £160k.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Sarah and Ian met on holiday in Spain and now want a second home in south-east Lanzarote for themselves and their family to enjoy. Jean Johansson shows them five great properties for their budget of £90,000.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps keen dancers Jane and James search for their dream holiday home on eastern Crete: a holidaymakers paradise full of stunning beaches with crystal-clear waters, archaeological sites and picturesque villages and towns.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander helps Tracy and Lee find a French holiday home in Lower Normandy, which they hope could one day become their permanent home, for their budget of £70k.

programme.title: Bill Baileys Vietnam Adventure
programme.desc: In Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous hub in Vietnam, Comedian Bill Bailey finds himself caught between wartime tunnels, endless traffic, and the genres of metal and folk.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
programme.desc: A seemingly unremarkable man in his 60s named Harold one day learns his old friend Queenie is dying. He embarks on a walk, only to keep going for 450 miles until he reaches Queenies hospice, much to the despair of his wife Maureen.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: Delve into the inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman and the macabre lengths the killer went to in attempting to conceal her death. Annette Smiths lodger reports that she has been missing for several weeks.

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. The police continue to probe the suspect and the interview becomes more and more chilling.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: The nations most romantic restaurant reopens with Fred Sirieix leading the way. Derek and June are a pair of music-loving romantics in their golden years. Mike, a widowed dad, searches for someone who understands the weight of grief.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: A young Londoner heads to Thailand to work in his dads bar, but gets seduced by life in the fast lane, until it all comes crashing down.

programme.title: Banged up Abroad
programme.desc: American David and his German friend Tom smuggle drugs from Afghanistan to Germany, but they are intercepted at the Turkish-Greek border. They have to serve their sentence in a Greek prison.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Steve is called to the scene of an overturned gas tanker that may be leaking highly flammable propane. Sean is asked to pick up an abandoned scaffolding lorry that is blocked in on a dead end street, locked and with no key.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Outback legend Steve Grahame is hauling three million dollars-worth of vital power generating equipment to an isolated community in the far North West of Australia - Kalumburu.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Its mid-afternoon in Perth and Dions super size convoy is pushing hard to get out of the city before sunset. And the fate of Steves trip hangs on one phone call to his mechanic.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Over the last three days, Steve Grahame has hauled 130 tonnes of freight a massive 2000 km, and his trucks taken a beating. Under the bonnet the news isnt good.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Turbo faces a journey that takes him directly through an area affected by a bush fire. Plus, owner operator Yogi faces the biggest week of his career. But how will he get on?

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Turbo gambles his truck, not to mention his life, by taking a route through devastatingly tough terrain. And Yogi races to beat a vital deadline on a make or break job.

programme.title: El Dorado
programme.desc: Gunslinger Cole Thornton rides into a small town to work for a cattle baron, but quits after learning that his would-be employer wants to drive a family off their land and obtain their water supply. Thornton leaves the situation unresolved, but a chance encounter takes him back to El Dorado, where he joins the sheriff in a range war.

programme.title: Rio Lobo
programme.desc: A former Union Army colonel sets out to settle some old scores and find the two Yankee traitors responsible for the loss of a gold shipment and the death of his best friend. His search leads him to a showdown in the town of Rio Lobo, where he finds a whole community under siege from their own sheriff and a mysterious landowner.

programme.title: Brannigan

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Claytons Towing rescues a truckload of milk that has run off the Bruce Highway. Wagga Truck Towing assists a grain trailer.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Claytons Towing must deal with tides and bull sharks when they have to salvage two yachts. Parks Towing rescue a sheep transport truck that has crashed into a railroad crossing in thick fog.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: Jessie from GRS Towing hauls an eight-tonne riverboat through three states. The Cool brothers have two hours to collect two electrical installation trucks.

programme.title: Extreme Tow Truckers
programme.desc: New South Wales is hit with the heaviest rain in 70 years, which leads to flooding. in Queensland, driver Mackie puts everything on the line while crossing the murky Mary River for a towing mission.

programme.title: Mud Mountain Haulers
programme.desc: Craig Lebeau and his team are pushed to the limit when their main driver gets stuck in a serious accident with a fully loaded truck. They wonder if the money is worth the risks.

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law
programme.desc: A man is behaving suspiciously and may have a weapon. Security guards Mark and Keiths number-one priority is to keep the public safe, so they put their lives on the line to corner him.

programme.title: Shoplifters: At War With The Law

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britains roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors catch a gang of car thieves red-handed. Elsewhere, officers clamp down on car crime at Essexs biggest shopping centre and help get the M25 moving after an accident.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: A Hollywood director tries to dupe Columbo when a studio becomes a murder scene.

programme.title: Columbo
programme.desc: Crime drama in which the rumpled lieutenant is invited to give a college lecture on criminology, but ends up investigating the murder of one of the institutions professors. programme.desc: When top network programmer Mark McAndrews gets promoted to a New York position, his chief assistant, ambitious Kay Freestone, expects to get his job - especially since theyre lovers. However, Mark doesnt think shes ready to handle it.

programme.desc: Relive the controversies that defined 1968, the `Year of Revolution. 1968 saw the ever-questing Beatles dabble in both commerce, with the ill-fated Apple Boutique, and mysticism, with a trip to India to meditate with Maharishi Mahesh Yogi.

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: When former hit man Jimmy Catto is released after serving 20 years for murder, a series of shootings take place which smack of Cattos handiwork. Taggart, who was a childhood friend of his, is not convinced that Jimmy is the culprit.

programme.title: How Do Animals Do That?

programme.title: One Hundred Great Paintings
programme.desc: This was the last of many hauntingly beautiful sketches and paintings Constable made of Salisbury Cathedral. He worked on it for years. During a bout of rheumatic fever, he propped the picture up against the foot of his bed. In the end, he was satisfied... At last, he wrote, I have got the Great Salisbury into the condition I wish to see it.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple in Norwich plan their ideal city bolthole. Martin is in desirable Fulham to see a flat with great potential. And in County Durham, theres a developer with a pretty big commute.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a leafy suburb of Hampshire, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits an unrivalled collection of Matchbox toy cars dating back to the 1950s. Collector Nigel Cooper reckons he has about 25,000 miniature motors, some he bought as a boy with his pocket money. Among his rarest models is a coronation coach and horses from 1953 with a surprising story. In neighbouring Surrey, two teddy-bear-loving teams compete for profits at a huge toy fair at Sandown Park Racecourse.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: National and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley celebrates International Womens Day with stories of inspiring women of faith, past and present. In St Pauls Cathedral, Kate meets the most Rev Dame Sarah Mullally ahead of her installation as the first female archbishop of Canterbury in history. Archbishop Sarah Mullally talks about her journey of faith, what her previous vocation as a nurse brings to her new role and how far the Church of England has come since 1994 when women were first ordained.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple currently living in Manchester are hoping to up sticks to the Cheshire countryside with their young daughter. They have requested a four bedroom, detached family home with a large, private garden, and Nicki Chapman has a very tempting selection of properties that suit the brief, as well as a mystery house that makes them reconsider their wish list.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Gladiators Ready! Bradley and Barney Walsh welcome back four more steel-nerved Contenders to battle the indomitable Gladiators as they compete for the last semi-final places.

programme.title: Spider-Man 3
programme.desc: Life is sweet for Peter Parker, MJ loves him, her career is taking off and Spidey-mania is sweeping New York. However, when an alien parasite bonds to his suit, its not long before the good times and Spidey turn bad. While his ugly ego is taking centre stage, MJ is dumped from her Broadway show, Harry remembers Spider-Mans role in his fathers death, and Peter is consumed by revenge.

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: Twelve months after exposing Correction in the most dramatic circumstances, whistleblower Rachel Carey is back, ready to launch Operation Veritas, a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. With the home secretary Isaac Turners support, the changes signal a new dawn in UK counter-terrorism policing. That is until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A couple in Norwich plan their ideal city bolthole. Martin is in desirable Fulham to see a flat with great potential. And in County Durham, theres a developer with a pretty big commute.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a leafy suburb of Hampshire, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits an unrivalled collection of Matchbox toy cars dating back to the 1950s. Collector Nigel Cooper reckons he has about 25,000 miniature motors, some he bought as a boy with his pocket money. Among his rarest models is a coronation coach and horses from 1953 with a surprising story. In neighbouring Surrey, two teddy-bear-loving teams compete for profits at a huge toy fair at Sandown Park Racecourse.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley celebrates International Womens Day with stories of inspiring women of faith, past and present. In St Pauls Cathedral, Kate meets the most Rev Dame Sarah Mullally ahead of her installation as the first female archbishop of Canterbury in history. Archbishop Sarah Mullally talks about her journey of faith, what her previous vocation as a nurse brings to her new role and how far the Church of England has come since 1994 when women were first ordained.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple currently living in Manchester are hoping to up sticks to the Cheshire countryside with their young daughter. They have requested a four bedroom, detached family home with a large, private garden, and Nicki Chapman has a very tempting selection of properties that suit the brief, as well as a mystery house that makes them reconsider their wish list.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Gladiators Ready! Bradley and Barney Walsh welcome back four more steel-nerved Contenders to battle the indomitable Gladiators as they compete for the last semi-final places.

programme.title: Spider-Man 3
programme.desc: Life is sweet for Peter Parker, MJ loves him, her career is taking off and Spidey-mania is sweeping New York. However, when an alien parasite bonds to his suit, its not long before the good times and Spidey turn bad. While his ugly ego is taking centre stage, MJ is dumped from her Broadway show, Harry remembers Spider-Mans role in his fathers death, and Peter is consumed by revenge.

programme.desc: Twelve months after exposing Correction in the most dramatic circumstances, whistleblower Rachel Carey is back, ready to launch Operation Veritas, a revolutionary new camera network capable of catching live deep-fakes in action. That is until a terror attack in central London sends shockwaves through the nation, leaving Rachel the one key eyewitness.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In a leafy suburb of Hampshire, Natasha Raskin Sharp visits an unrivalled collection of Matchbox toy cars dating back to the 1950s. Collector Nigel Cooper reckons he has about 25,000 miniature motors, some he bought as a boy with his pocket money. Among his rarest models is a coronation coach and horses from 1953 with a surprising story. In neighbouring Surrey, two teddy-bear-loving teams compete for profits at a huge toy fair at Sandown Park Racecourse.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley celebrates International Womens Day with stories of inspiring women of faith, past and present. In St Pauls Cathedral, Kate meets the most Rev Dame Sarah Mullally ahead of her installation as the first female archbishop of Canterbury in history. Archbishop Sarah Mullally talks about her journey of faith, what her previous vocation as a nurse brings to her new role and how far the Church of England has come since 1994 when women were first ordained.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple currently living in Manchester are hoping to up sticks to the Cheshire countryside with their young daughter. programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommys road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldnt train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity: a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi, and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mum out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but after completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp
programme.desc: A British epic about the life and loves of a reactionary army officer, Clive Candy VC, told episodically in flashback. From his dashing career in the Boer War of 1902, the film follows Candy through marriage during the First World War to the Blitz of 1943, when he searches London for his lost youth and loves.

programme.title: Angels One Five
programme.desc: A Hurricane pilot falls victim to the wrath of his squadron after a crash-landing, and must battle to win favour, as well as battling the Luftwaffe.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin is at Huddersfield Town Hall in West Yorkshire. Experts Nick Davies and Christina Trevanion search for antiques to take to auction, and Paul delves into the areas coal mining history at the National Coal Mining Museum for England.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Ally and son Noah apply the salon treatment to go head-to-head with mainland breeders.

programme.title: Mens Six Nations
programme.desc: All the talking points and in-depth analysis from Round 4 of the Six Nations. England face a tricky trip to Rome against Italy, Ireland host Wales in Dublin and Scotland welcome France to Murrayfield.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The roadshow is at Pitzhanger Manor in Ealing, west London, where finds include a quirky 18th-century Harlequinade childrens flip book, a vintage trainer collection and a flying helmet from the Battle of Britain. Alastair Chandler comes across a unique pocket watch that symbolises a familys story on the eve of the Second World War, and Jon Baddeley is enthralled by the story behind a medal awarded to a suffragette.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: With the dominant males gone, trouble looms as intruder lions close in on the Xudum prides young cubs. Leopard Lediba searches for her missing cub after a tense encounter, and Pobe the cheetah must defend her familys food from bigger cats.

programme.title: Forensics: The Real CSI
programme.desc: Multiple cameras follow serious crime investigations in real time, revealing the crucial role cutting edge forensic science now plays in bringing criminals to justice.

programme.title: Platoon
programme.desc: A vivid and harrowing depiction of war which follows a 19-year-old college dropout who signs up for service in Vietnam in September 1967, and finds himself plunged into the bloody chaos of life as a GI on the front line. The winner of four Academy Awards, including Best Director and Best Picture, this is an uncompromising and semi-autobiographical study of men at war from writer-director Oliver Stone.

programme.desc: Nikki, Jack, Harriet and Kit investigate the apparent suicide of a British-Chinese activist and must navigate complex international tensions.

programme.desc: Motoring maverick Henry Cole goes through over a decades worth of footage, featuring oily chaos, revisiting the best barn finds, examining the worst breakdowns and biggest rebuilds.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: The team find a Rolls-Royce that lures them to Yorkshire. Although it is old, the car is very economical, and the high quality can still be felt.

programme.desc: Rick and Chum kick it with a rare pair of Pumas from 1968. Next, Chum is blown away by a fan from the 1940s. Later, Rick reads into a collection of letters from historical figures, but its some lab notes by Marie Curie that really leave him glowing.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Corey has an appetite for a deal when a Guns N Roses collector box rocks into the shop.

programme.desc: The guys leave an auction empty handed and try to find new merchandise at a yard sale, and questionable antique tennis rackets and an ancient special effects box may not earn them anything.

programme.title: Auction Hunters
programme.desc: A ruthless store owner tries to stop Ton and Allen from scoring new items in Santa Barbara so they fight back, and the guys uncover a rare antique air gun and a vintage wooden boat built for speed.

programme.title: Auction Hunters

programme.title: Storage Wars

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Join the CBBC crew for all your favourite programmes. Tune into CBBC every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep: Adventures from Mossy Bottom
programme.desc: Shaun is the centre of adventure and excitement at Mossy Bottom Farm, making mischief for the Farmer and his long-suffering sheep dog, Bitzer.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Its staff appreciation day at Danger HQ and Squawk and Danger Mouse have two very different ideas on where to go. Penfold has the deciding vote so the pair are busy trying to convince Penfold to pick their choice. With Penfold busy inventing tasks so they can earn his favour no one has noticed that a jam spillage in the professors experiment has brought a sticky surprise to life - a super-sized jam monster which might just ruin all of their plans.

programme.title: Dennis and Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.sub-title: Red and Black to the Future

programme.desc: Dad takes up ventriloquism as a hobby. The kids must find a way to stop Dad from embarrassing himself as he tries to showcase his new talent around town.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: News magazine keeping young viewers up to date with the latest stories and events happening at home and abroad.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: When Mr Krabs puts in an open kitchen, SpongeBob gets stage fright and has to invent a new persona.

programme.title: Grizzy and The Lemmings
programme.desc: Solar panels do not generate enough electricity to charge Grizzys TV. He sees the Lemmings playing with fireflies that could make his panels work, and tries to distract the Lemmings and capture the fireflies.

programme.desc: A bunch of bananas, door handle, fridge and tortilla are among the items get the Sketchy treatment!

programme.title: Duck and Frog

programme.desc: Gark brings home a bedraggled street cat, then discovers he was Bettys first cat. While living with her lots of accidents befell him, and he is out for revenge.

programme.title: Bros in Control

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Packed full of amazing celeb guests, epic challenges and lols galore!

programme.title: 500 Words
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp host the final of the 2026 500 Words childrens writing competition from Windsor Castle, invited by Her Majesty the Queen. With special performances from Paddington the Musical and West End Legend Marisha Wallace. Plus, Jodie Whittaker, Big Zuu and Bradley and Barney Walsh help reveal the winning stories.

programme.title: My Mum Tracy Beaker
programme.desc: Jess loves her life with her mum Tracy, until famous footballer Sean Godfrey arrives. So when they move into Seans mansion, how will the millionaire life suit the Beaker girls?

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: The Doctors experiment on their muscles to show how they work. Dr Chris meets some wriggly maggots that are helping to heal a sore foot and Dr Xand goes on call with an emergency ambulance team. Meanwhile over in Accident and Emergency, one patient has some grit in his head after a fall in the playground and another has injured his neck after performing a karate kick.

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: A show celebrating some of Historys greatest, and also most formidable females. We begin with ancient tales of the Scythians - incredible warrior women who were part of the inspiration for the wonder women that were more familiar with today.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.sub-title: Sizzling Monks, Algorithms and Weather Frogs
programme.desc: Dr Big Brain has an electrical short circuit, Marie Curie is wowed by a rocket man, some of the great women of science are celebrated, and 200 monks get electrocuted!

programme.title: The Next Step

But before she can move up in the world, shes going to need some help from Duggee and the Squirrels to move her many belongings up the hill. Snail cant do it alone, so Duggee teaches the Squirrels the best ways that they can get moving! They band together, cradling items and shifting furniture.

programme.title: Hey Duggee

Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasnt loud at all but impossibly quiet. As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in.

programme.desc: Banku, his mother, Anjali Sharma and father move in to their new house - the Nath villa, unaware of the fact that the house is inhabited by a ghost. It is soon discovered the ghost is not too happy with his new housemates. However the ghost quickly befriends Banku, and he must uncover the reason why his ghostly friend is stuck and help him to attain salvation.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: A Kentucky homestead built in a ghost town drowns in floods. The Raneys revive the original barn, devise a livestock escape pod and create a floating greenhouse.

programme.title: Homestead Rescue

programme.title: Homestead Rescue
programme.desc: An early 20s couple struggle to keep their homestead afloat in torrential Texas weather. But the Raneys face a tense and daunting rescue to save the rain-devastated cabin and soggy livestock shelters under threat of storms and tornado warnings.

programme.title: Moonshiners

programme.title: Diesel Brothers

programme.title: Hoffman Family Gold
programme.desc: Todd Hoffman banks his future mining career on turning around a rundown mine 80 miles north of Nome, Alaska, where unforgiving weather, rookie crews and beat up equipment are affecting the mines ability to keep operations going.

programme.title: Hoffman Family Gold
programme.desc: When Alaskan temperatures plunge, Todd and the team are caught by surprise. Their biggest wash plant is frozen and the Hoffmans rush to thaw out their other plants before theyre iced out for the season.

programme.title: Overhaulin
programme.desc: When his father died seven years ago, Merrick inherited his prized possession, a 67 Charger. Merricks mum calls in the Overhaulin team to fix it up.

programme.title: Overhaulin
programme.desc: Matt adores his Model A Ford but has no time to restore it himself. His wife Aimee contacts the `Overhaulin" guys to surprise him.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.desc: Bruce gets hold of an extremely rare, and valuable, German Panther tank, and its original undercoat is more pink than camouflage. But getting parts is a struggle.

programme.title: Combat Dealers
programme.desc: Bruce is on a WWI treasure hunt, finding a mysterious soldiers medal from the Battle of the Somme. Plus, he finds the worlds most expensive football.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: The boys are in Oxfordshire, and Marks shed is more of a boneyard for giants. Everything Henry and Sam look at here is oversized and likely over-priced, but Henry likes a tractor in blue and Sam falls for a Midget racer in a hedge.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around peoples sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, the pair are in Somerset where they discover an amazing collection of Norton motorbikes, Italian mopeds and French go-karts. Moving on to Hertfordshire, they find vintage cars, obscure tractors and rare British bikes from the 1950s. Everything is going well, but when the haggling starts, things dont turn out quite how anyone expected.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Today the Shed and Buried boys are moving on to Hertfordshire, where they find vintage cars, obscure tractors and rare British bikes from the 1950s. Its all going well, but when the haggling starts, things dont turn out quite how anyone expected.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around peoples sheds and garages in search of automotive bargains. Today, the pair are in the Cotswolds where they discover sheds full of stationary engines, obscure agricultural implements and some very surprising collections of memorabilia. Moving on up to Sussex, they go behind the scenes at one of Britains most famous bike shops, and find in its dark corners and dusty attics, two extraordinary hidden gems.

programme.title: HMS Ark Royal
programme.desc: HMS Ark Royal is taking part in large-scale war games with the US Navy. Her mission is to project air power and oversee the landing of Royal Marines into the designated warzone.

programme.title: HMS Ark Royal
programme.desc: After nearly five months since she departed on the Auriga deployment, HMS Ark Royal is welcomed back home into Portsmouth, but not before a full-scale battle damage exercise.

programme.title: Bullet Points
programme.desc: How did America recover so quickly from the attack on Pearl Harbor and achieve supremacy over the Japanese Empire in the Pacific?

programme.title: Combat Dealers
programme.desc: Bruce splurges on a rare Sherman tank in Ohio. But before he flogs it he wants to boost its authenticity and value, so the twins consult a WWII tank veteran for advice.

programme.title: Combat Dealers
programme.desc: Bruce is tasked with creating a display based on the French Resistance: a parachute basket filled with items the RAF would have dropped to the Resistance in WWII.

programme.title: Combat Dealers
programme.desc: Bruce heads to Ypres looking for items from WWI, finding a German trench mortar. He also goes for a ride in a WWI-era British tank - one of the first tanks ever used in combat.

programme.title: Worlds Greatest Motorcycle Rides
programme.desc: Crossing the deserts en-route to California, Henry Cole is greeted by stifling heat and zero humidity, not to mention the assortment of UFO hunters, gunslingers, eccentric millionaires and crazy rednecks.

programme.title: Worlds Greatest Motorcycle Rides
programme.desc: Henry Cole completes his journey, defying the elements. Will he think Route 66 still rates as one of the greatest rides on earth or is its magical allure slowly becoming a thing of the past?

programme.title: Worlds Greatest Motorcycle Rides
programme.desc: Could the Worlds Greatest Motorcycle Ride be only 37 miles long? Well it might not be a gruelling adventure but the Isle of Man is home to the most famous road race in the world; the Isle of Man TT. For presenter Henry Cole this circuit is motorcycling Mecca but what has the Isle of Man got to offer away from the racing?

programme.title: Blowing Up History
programme.desc: Experts use cutting-edge technology to investigate a lost city buried in the Sahara. Archaeologists are uncovering the citys forgotten ruins and dark secrets.

programme.title: Blowing Up History
programme.desc: When Pompeii was destroyed by an apocalyptic volcano, another town was destroyed, too - Herculaneum. It is one of historys most perfectly preserved towns.

programme.title: Blowing Up History
programme.desc: The Roman Empire built Hadrians Wall to guard its frontiers. New discoveries reveal who the Romans were trying to keep out with this 72-mile-long megastructure.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: French polisher Alex is tasked with restoring a huge clock. Ruth revamps a pair of golden candle sticks, and Cliff works on a century-old vessel.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: Metal worker Ted is challenged to repurpose a rusty railway signal light. Alex restores a headless carousel animal, while Craig fixes up a lounge chair.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: The Restorers
programme.desc: Metal worker Ted gets to work revamping a 19th century Parisian streetlight. Plus, taxidermist Chris uses his skills to restore a tope shark before putting it in a beautiful display case.

programme.desc: Phil is distracted by a hot neighbour when hes supposed to be looking after his son, Mitchell and Cameron take Lily to their Mommy and Me class, and its father and son time for Manny and Jay.

programme.title: Hollyoaks: Omnibus
programme.desc: After months of torment, Dodger finally has his chance to expose Sully, but Sullys ready to fight dirty... Vicky bonds with Prince, Sienna makes a tough decision and Donny faces the truth.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tubes naughty little sister that features a variety of cats doing strange things, some soldiers stuck in the sand and a dog balancing on a rope.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Celebrating the freaky, frenetic and funny side of family life.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Claire plans a baby shower for Haley at Jays house, but this interferes with Jays plan of hosting a Super Bowl party.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Lily has reached a womanly milestone that Cam and Mitch are not prepared for - at all - so they call in reinforcements.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The guys share their biggest fears when they crash Jays annual hiking trip. Back home, the girls get their DIY on as they build Haleys new nursery.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil feels emasculated when Bill wows Dylan and Luke with heroic tales. He gets them over for some male bonding, but it goes rather wrong.

programme.title: Sunday Brunch
programme.desc: Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: Short cheeky clips feature ducks going up an escalator, animal impressions, a few narrow escapes on a snowboard and rally driving. Plus, some interesting food clips with an energy drink house prank, and a butcher rap.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.sub-title: Revisit Hour 1 - Sante La Brea, Finn McCool, Giuseppi
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay hits the road to help struggling restaurants all over the United States turn their luck around.

programme.title: Ramsays Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Providence, Rhode Island to visit Down City, a restaurant with a hot-headed owner. If Gordon can stop this restaurant closing down he must first break through the tough-talking Abby, Down Citys tyrannical owner.

programme.title: Kindergarten Cop
programme.desc: Comic tale in which a tough cop is forced to go undercover as a kindergarten teacher. In his new role, he must track down a mother and son who are the only witnesses against a drug-dealing criminal, who is also their husband and father. The cop uses his brutal methods to negotiate the mundane problems of child control.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Boy Who Knew Too Much
programme.desc: Bart is the only witness when Mayor Quimbys nephew Freddy is falsely accused of assaulting a French butler, but testifying at the trial means admitting that he was skipping school.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Grampa and Mr Burns compete for the affections of Marges mother. Can she resist Mr Burns marriage proposal?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer reveals Marges secrets when he teaches an adult education class in marriage and parenting.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The City of New York vs Homer Simpson
programme.desc: Homer takes the family to New York after Barney leaves the car illegally parked at the World Trade Center.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The real Seymour Skinner returns to Springfield, revealing that Principal Skinner - whose real name is Armin Tamzarian - is an impostor who stole his identity during the Vietnam War. A disgraced Tamzarian leaves Springfield to live on the streets of Capital City, but the real Seymour Skiner proves harder for Springfield to stomach.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bong Joon-hos "Parasite," Bambis revenge and much, much more are featured in the annual Halloween special.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Evergreen Terrace is overrun with traffic and Moe has a fateful choice to make.

programme.title: The Transporter
programme.desc: For the right price, ex-Special Forces officer Frank Martin will take anything anywhere. He just has three rules: no changes to the plan; no real names; and no opening the package - until one package starts to move. Inside it is the beautiful Lai, and although Martin delivers her to the unscrupulous Darren `Wall Street Bettencourt, his conscience soon gets the better of him and he decides to rescue Lai.

Together, they must save mankind from the wicked enchantress Morgana and her army of supernatural warriors.

programme.title: Mary, Queen of Scots

The technical challenge is Pauls favourite sweet treat - iced fingers. Finally, the bakers must produce a batch of three different types of pastries or croissants.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins host the grand final where Holly, Jo and Mary-Anne face their biggest challenge yet - baking for `The Great British Bake Off street party. None of them have baked on this scale before. Amongst their offerings are mille-feuille, mini Victoria sponges, strawberry and cream meringue nests and miniature strawberry and rhubarb cheesecakes. Who of the three will take home the trophy?

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: Chef James Martin searches for more great American cakes. He visits that American institution, the diner, for a traditional pancake breakfast.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James is invited into the home of Connecticut local, who makes him a Jewish holiday honey cake, before lending a hand at a local cooking class.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: More cake adventures as James ventures into the kitchens of a Boston hotel to find out why Boston Cream Pie is called the state dessert of Massachusetts.

programme.title: James Martins United Cakes of America
programme.desc: In Boston, James visits a local distillery to meet two brothers who are reviving the citys history for rum making, before sampling a boozy rum cake.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Mississippi Adventure
programme.desc: Si and Dave visit the epicentre of Cajun culture, experiencing great food and great people in Lafayette. They tuck in to crayfish and groove at a jam session.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Its all about speed as Ree Drummond shares four recipes she can make PDQ -- pretty darn quickly.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Its question time on the ranch and Ree is covering topics ranging from recipes and romance, to fashion and ranch favourites. Her answers include three meat, three bean, three chilli chilli, shrimp mango lettuce cups, and linguine with clam sauce.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Avocado Hollandaise and Speedy Sausage Pizza
programme.desc: Jamie rustles up his restaurant-quality avocado hollandaise and then he gets his son Buddy involved in cooking a different pizza for all the family to enjoy.

programme.title: Jamies Easy Meals for Every Day
programme.sub-title: Chicken and Pepper Curry and Broccoli and Cheese Pierogi
programme.desc: Jamie demonstrates an easy traybake, chicken and pepper jalfrezi, where the oven does all the hard work. Then he shares his version of pierogi dumplings, stuffed with broccoli and cheese.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Inas on an Italian adventure. Classic bolognese gets an easy makeover with fast to prepare, and cook, week-night bolognese. Then its a Tuscan classic of roasted sausage and grapes.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Honey vanilla pound cake; Atlantic Mist cheese and apples.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Its semi-final time and the four remaining bakers must prove they are worth a place in the final. The signature challenge requires them to make a baked, layered, mousse cake. The technical challenge is Pauls favourite sweet treat - iced fingers. Finally, the bakers must produce a batch of three different types of pastries or croissants.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins host the grand final where Holly, Jo and Mary-Anne face their biggest challenge yet - baking for `The Great British Bake Off street party. None of them have baked on this scale before. Amongst their offerings are mille-feuille, mini Victoria sponges, strawberry and cream meringue nests and miniature strawberry and rhubarb cheesecakes. Who of the three will take home the trophy?

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Adam Richman Eats Italy
programme.desc: Foodie Adam Richman embarks on a delicious culinary adventure across Italy. He visits the places that are home to the most iconic Italian dishes.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge Secrets of the Pub Kitchen
programme.desc: Tom Kerridge picks popular items on the menus at modern British pubs and gives them his own twist. He takes inspiration from modern gastropubs to create simple, contemporary, and delicious recipes.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge Secrets of the Pub Kitchen
programme.desc: At Toms Michelin-starred pub, the team prepare a special dish of rotisserie beef with mushroom tart and walnut ketchup, and a luxurious rice pudding.

programme.title: Man v. Food Best Of
programme.desc: A rack of baby back ribs; juicy pulled pork; a six-pound BBQ sandwich with fries.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: Casey Webb travels down to Georgia to take on a four-pound pulled pork sandwich called the Pigzilla.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri is travelling the country and digging into awesome bird and beef. In Longmont, a fried chicken emporium serves up their specialty, along with unique chicharrones.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri rolls into a tucked-away panini paradise in Chehalis, Wash.; a Korean spot in Brentwood, Tenn., gets spicy with their chicken and bulgogi; in Houston, a barbecue joint throws Tex-Mex into the mix with brisket tacos and jalapeno sausage.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieris checking out how stellar sandwiches stack up. He hits the road for elevated meatloaf and creative chicken trifecta in Omaha, NE, dynamite dip and fall-off-the-bone pork in Daphne, AL, and bomb bao with a righteous Reuben in Stamford, CT.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri road trips across America to check out classic greasy spoons. With a new generation of owners and delicious homemade food, these are the kind of places that make America special.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Snooki, Super Anthony and a Ship
programme.desc: Buddy takes a trip to the Seaport Museum in New York to get inspiration for a nautical-themed cake. Meanwhile, a celebrity stops by Carlos to order a cake for her mum.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.sub-title: Sweet Sixteen, Stars and a Saber Sword
programme.desc: Buddy makes a cake for the 100th anniversary of the Cadet Chapel at West Point. He also makes a Hollywood-themed cake for Mauros daughters red carpet Sweet 16 party.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and South East chefs are cooking all four courses again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by Mary Gwynn, author of the official WI centenary cookbook. Together theyll decide which chef goes through to represent the region in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central Regions finest chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge with their starters. With big boots to fill, Angela Hartnetts protege Pip is the only newcomer in the kitchen. Will she beat the boys to claim victory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three Central Region chefs - newcomer Pip Lacey, 4th time competitor Richard Bainbridge and returning chef Jason Hodnett - fight it out for the fish course. Pip is hoping to outclass her rivals with a smoked haddock dish inspired by her Grandma.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with three Central Region chefs - Pip Lacey, Richard Bainbridge and Jason Hodnett. Once again Jason is taking risks with his unusual collection of ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the Central Region chefs to impress, with the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges so with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central Region chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyll be joined by former chair of the Womens Institute Helen Carey, who will help decide who will have the glory of representing the region in the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course, and 8 of the finest chefs in the country are battling it out to plate up impeccable dishes worthy of the Womens Institute. The judges are joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, but after Oliver reveals that this year it will be much tougher to make it onto the judges shortlist, who will keep their cool and secure a place?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle for the fish course, and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by a WI president, Kristy Bowen, who knows the impeccable standards the Institute will expect.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day, the half-way point in Finals Week, and those chefs who have yet to get a dish on the shortlist are feeling the heat. With only dishes judged to be perfect in with a chance at reaching the WI banquet, the standards are higher than ever before.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for dessert, and the last chance for the 8 regional champions to get a dish in contention for the final banquet. Those whove failed to make the judges shortlist so far are going all out. Joining the judges is author of the WI centenary cookbook Mary Gwynn.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The banquet is finally here. Its the culmination of months of hard work as the winning chefs strive to plate up perfect dishes for the ladies of the Womens Institute, at Londons historic Drapers Hall where pioneers of the WI once gathered.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their summery starters. A dish celebrating childhood summer memories takes on a complicated courgette dish and a technically tricky take on the cucumber sandwich.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more intense as all the chefs take risks with their dishes. Mike Reid, Executive Chef at M Restaurants in London, shocks with when he pairs his fish with some highly unusual flavours.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a smoke filled kitchen as the three chefs prepare their summer themed mains in the hope of impressing the veteran judge. Selin Kiazim is in her element, cooking a feast inspired by the summer barbecues she enjoyed with her family.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are feeling the pressure as they attempt their technical summer desserts. With only the two highest scorers going through to tomorrows regional final, who will be sent home?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London and the South East chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges: nd for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs have been challenged to create outstanding dishes that capture a `taste of summer. Their menus must reflect the tastes, smells and colours of everybodys favourite time of year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and with a seafood specialist in the kitchen the competition is stepping up a gear. Tom is hoping hell be able to elevate the humble mackerel to the standards required for the Wimbledon banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With a refined summer barbecue going up against a traditional Cornish speciality and a historic Wimbledon related recipe, who will come out on top? Andy Clatworthy is using his passion for historical cooking to reinvent a dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: All three chefs are honouring Wimbledon and the British summer with their technically challenging strawberry desserts. Only the two highest scorers will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs compete for a place in the national finals. To get there theyll have to impress the formidable panel of judges: Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and for the first time this year, broadcaster and restaurateur Andi Oliver.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tom Parker is eager to impress with a salad that includes 10 elements. Meanwhile, Paul Askew, hopes his traditional techniques will triumph over his younger competitors, and Ellis Barrie is taking a risk with a creative cucumber and oyster starter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the atmosphere in the kitchen gets even tenser as the three newcomers begin to understand the perfection and skill the veteran chef demands. Ellis Barrie attempts to create the flavour of a summer barbecue.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and the pressure is intense in the kitchen. In the hope of impressing the veteran judge, Paul Askew is serving an extremely personal main course based on his mothers favourite food.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Paul cant afford to make any mistakes and is pushing himself with a technically challenging baked alaska.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. The guest judge is Greg Rusedski, former British number one, who understands the high standards expected at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning banquet champion Tommy Banks takes on two ambitious newcomers Danny Parker and Josh Overington. They start by finding out whois the surprise veteran judge and who will be marking their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated, the three chefs are cooking their fish courses. Josh Overington, creates a decadent dish inspired by the prestige of the Wimbledon Championships.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are cooking their main courses and are all taking risks to try and win a place at the banquet. Danny Parker, is trying to recreate the flavours of an American barbecue but the veteran chef is dubious about one of his ingredients.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. All the chefs are using unusual ingredients in their desserts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the North East must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver, and guest Judy Murray.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the Scottish regional heats and returning chefs to the competition Michael Bremner and Ally McGrath battle ambitious newcomer Angela Malik for a place in the national finals. This time they are cooking their starters.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The atmosphere in the kitchen gets even more heated and the veteran chef is expecting perfection from the fish course. Ally McGrath creates an inventive dish based on his summer memories of rock pooling in the River Tweed.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Angela Malik is in her element, cooking a tiffin box based on memories of summer picnics. Michael Bremner is taking a huge risk, and Ally is creating an inventive dish based on a summer barbeque.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges and the pressure in the kitchen is intense. Angela Malik is cooking a technically challenging dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two Scottish chefs must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver. They are joined by guest judge Gary Parsons.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Determined newcomers Paul Croasdale and Nick Brodie take on returning chef Phil Carmichael for a place in the national finals. Its Phils third time in the competition and this year hes determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The pressure in the kitchen is high as the chefs prepare their fish courses. Paul Croasdale is trying to recreate the elegance of Wimbledon with luxury ingredients such as king crab and scallops.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the main course and all three chefs are cooking Welsh lamb in hope of impressing. Phil is taking a risk with barbecued lamb and in an attempt to elevate a humble barbecue to banquet level, hes serving it with many accompaniments.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the dessert course and the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges so all three chefs are feeling the pressure. Phil Carmichael creates a tennis ball dessert that combines two British classics, Eton mess and trifle.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two winning chefs from the Wales region must cook their entire taste of summer menus again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges and guest judge Dan Bloxham, Wimbledons Master of Ceremonies.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With two spots left in the National Finals, its the turn of the Central chefs to battle it out. Pip Lacey, head chef at the Michelin starred Murano in London, tried her luck in the competition two years ago. Shes takes on two newcomers.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With the chefs hoping to impress, they each take risks. Pip Lacey combines lobster with strawberry in a tribute to strawberries and cream. Ryan Simpsons also using lobster in his recreation of a BBQ and has invented a technique to create his chips.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today the chefs cook their main courses and have very different takes on the Taste of Summer brief. Ryan Simpson hopes to elevate a ploughmans lunch into a dish worthy of the banquet, and Nick Deverell-Smith celebrates a meat he thinks is underused.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Dessert is the last chance for the chefs to secure a place cooking for the judges. Pip Laceys pineapple dessert uses coriander. Nick Deverell-Smith also pays tribute to the pineapple, creating a dessert featuring a rice pudding made with cous-cous.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. They must impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort, Andi Oliver and guest judge Jordanne Whiley, one of Britains most successful tennis players.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs meet their surprise veteran judge and cook their starters. Eddie is hoping to do better than last year, Joery is hoping to impress with a tribute to Richard Krajicek, and Tommy is creating a warm salad.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Joery Castel is looking to triumph by combing mackerel and cucumber. Tommy Heaney hopes his Murray Mound is a fitting tribute to Andi Murray, Eddie Attwell is creating five different strawberry elements to accompany the scallops in his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Tommy Heaneys BBQ dish is a surf and turf combination, Joery Castels aiming to recreate a more American style BBQ and Eddie Attwells taking a risk with an unusual meat.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs whove made it through cook their four courses again. Theyre hoping to impress the formidable panel of judges Oliver Peyton, Matthew Fort and Andi Oliver who are joined this week by tennis greatest entertainer, Mansour Bahrami.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: With Finals Week ahead of them, judge Andi Oliver first summons the chefs to Wimbledon to take a look at where they could be cooking if they win. The judges are joined by guest judge Sue Barker, host of the Wimbledon coverage for more than 20 years.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.desc: In small-town upstate New York, a young mother loses her five-year old son whos battled a medical condition that has plagued him since birth; doctors claim his death is not from a natural cause, and investigators soon suspect foul play.

programme.title: Murder Chose Me
programme.desc: A protective grandfather is found beaten to death on the side of the road. After an investigation begins, news of a recent incident involving a birthday gift leads Demery to the most unexpected of killers.

programme.title: People Magazine Investigates
programme.desc: In 1970s Detroit, a murder rocks an African-American neighbourhood. Police think theyve got the killers - but it will take 47 years for the truth to emerge.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Two men had been best friends since growing up together in California. What caused their relationship to break down in a devastating and bloody way?

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: 19-year-old Logan Schiendelman vanishes after a night out. When authorities find no evidence of foul play, they are forced to examine his familys controversial past.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: When transgender teen Sage Smith goes missing, the investigation turns up evidence that forces detectives to look closer into Sages inner circle.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: I Wished My Son Were Dead
programme.desc: It has been more than 25 years, yet Joyce Alexander is still terrified of her son, William Patrick Alexander; she fears he holds her responsible for his horrifying crime, and is convinced he is plotting his revenge from prison.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: He Almost Got Away With It
programme.desc: The heart-stopping accounts from people sharing their homes with loved ones, who become killers.

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: Dalhart, Texas, 2017. Joel Frazier is the proud father of two former rodeo queens, Camie and Jamie Frazier. Suddenly he goes missing. The town rumour mill is busy, and the hostility between the women in his life is mounting, but no one can find Joel.

programme.title: The Murder of Gabby Petito: A Faking It Special
programme.desc: Experts in body language, linguistics and forensic psychology are followed as they examine footage and interviews of individuals linked to the murder of Gabby Petito by her fiance Brian Laundrie.

programme.title: A Killer Among Friends
programme.desc: A gunman kills a University of Kentucky football player at his birthday party. Despite a house full of witnesses, no one saw the shooter. As police investigate, suspicion closes in on his closest friends who were there that night.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.desc: A mother awakes to find her two sons with their throats cut; their aunts boyfriend confesses to his mother, and she wears a wire to capture her son; as he admits his guilt, its clear how a psychopathic rage led to violence.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.sub-title: Make Sure You Get My Good Side
programme.desc: A road rage incident turns deadly as an elderly man is beaten to death by a teenager who claims he was acting in self-defense; detectives not only find holes in his story but uncover that he is a remorseless and manipulative psychopath.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: Trapped in a House of Torture
programme.desc: Tammy Moores loyalty to her mother, Marie, is absolute; when police arrest Marie for murder, Tammy lies to protect her; a look back at the horrific events of her youth forces Tammy to see her mother in a new, terrifying light.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.sub-title: My Brother Let the Evil In
programme.desc: Christina Thomas believes her brother, John, the first time he nearly kills a woman; she forgives him when he murders another; but when it happens again, she wonders why she trusted him and how she missed the signs he was really devil in disguise.

programme.title: Evil Online
programme.desc: High school student Brandon Wentzell falls for Clarissa Chistiakov online. However, the course of virtual love doesnt run smooth as the past collides with a toxic present.

programme.title: Evil Online
programme.desc: Beverly Carter is a 50-year-old internet savvy real-estate agent. But when she goes to meet a prospective buyer at a remote house, she never returns.

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime
programme.desc: What exposed Tracie Andrews lie that a road rage attack killed her lover? Mike Gifford-Hulls TV plea for his missing wife Farsi hides the truth.

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime

programme.desc: In Michigan, a mother is murdered by her jealous ex-boyfriend. Friends and family examine home video footage and question if there were any warning signs.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: In Knoxville, Tennessee, a seemingly perfect couple are secretly living a nightmare - the wife if found guilty of soliciting her new partner to kill her former husband.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: When Tyarra Williams vanishes, tips suggest she may have fallen prey to human trafficking. It is possible some in the community could know more than they are letting on about her disappearance.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: The heart-stopping accounts from people sharing their homes with loved ones, who become killers.

programme.title: Betrayed
programme.desc: 20-year-old Christian, Missy Grubaugh, is saving herself for marriage. But Missys innocent charms attract the wrong men with deadly results.

programme.title: Betrayed

programme.title: Where Murder Lies
